purpose: Compact context index for external projects and local skills. Use this before loading repository dumps.

projects.name: Scrapling
projects.token_strategy: Use utils.scrapling_adapter.scrape_text for narrow page extraction; do not paste scraped pages into prompts unless summarized first.
use_for: Collecting compact market/news/page text for downstream signal parsing
avoid_for: Trading execution
Long unattended crawls without rate limits
Fetching secrets or authenticated pages

projects.name: Hermes Agent
projects.token_strategy: Use as architecture reference for memory/tools/skills loops; keep only design decisions in continuous_improvement/knowledge_base.
avoid_for: Direct runtime dependency until a bounded adapter is implemented
Replacing the existing risk and execution engine

projects.name: PraisonAI
projects.token_strategy: Use only for bounded multi-agent workflows; pass compact task specs and file paths instead of whole-file context.
avoid_for: Production trading decisions without deterministic guardrails
Unbounded autonomous code changes

projects.name: Meta-Agent-with-More-Agents
projects.token_strategy: Use the meta-agent delegation pattern only as a planning pattern; delegate by file ownership and explicit output contract.
avoid_for: Duplicating local CI skills
Large context fan-out without a single compact synthesis

projects.name: Solana Jupiter Arbitrage Bot
projects.token_strategy: Use as external design reference for Jupiter quote/swap arbitrage only; keep Polymarket CLOB execution isolated unless an explicit Solana adapter is added.
avoid_for: Directly mixing Solana private keys into Vault trading path without a new secret policy
Executing Solana swaps from the Polymarket CLOB bot
Assuming token prices map to binary CLOB probabilities
required_guardrails: Separate Solana wallet secrets from CLOB secrets
Paper/simulation mode first
Explicit profitability model with fees, slippage, and priority fees
No PROD swap path without ALLOW_SOLANA_PROD=true and authenticated control plane

projects.name: LLM Council
projects.token_strategy: Use config/llm_council.json and MCP build_llm_council_plan before any live model call.
use_for: Anonymized cross-review of difficult design or risk questions
Chairman synthesis for compact final decisions
avoid_for: Sending secrets, raw logs, Telegram updates, or private keys to external providers
Direct trading execution
Unbounded model fan-out without max_models
required_guardrails: Dry-run plans by default
OpenRouter key must come from environment or Vault
Council output is advisory only and must pass deterministic project validators

projects.name: MiroFish
projects.license: AGPL-3.0 upstream; do not vendor upstream code without license review.
projects.token_strategy: Use config/mirofish.json and build_mirofish_simulation_plan to create compact simulation briefs before any model calls.
use_for: Pre-trade research briefs that remain advisory
avoid_for: Direct trading execution
Sending secrets, raw logs, or private Telegram updates to external models
Large unbounded simulations without round and agent caps
Copying AGPL upstream code directly into this repo without approval
required_guardrails: Planning only by default
Bound rounds and agent counts
Finance scenarios must include fees, slippage, liquidity, latency, and manipulation assumptions
Simulation output must pass HMM, risk engine, ledger, and execution mode checks before action

projects.name: Graphify
projects.token_strategy: Build or update graphify-out first, then answer codebase questions with graph queries, GRAPH_REPORT.md highlights, and narrow file reads instead of dumping the repository.
use_for: Finding related modules without loading full files
Low-token onboarding for future AI agents
avoid_for: Trading execution
Indexing secrets, encrypted user data, logs, or runtime databases
Treating inferred graph edges as audited facts without checking source files
required_guardrails: Respect .graphifyignore before indexing
Prefer graphify query/path/explain outputs before reading broad source files
Keep graphify-out generated artifacts out of prompts unless summarized

projects.name: Claude-Mem
projects.token_strategy: Use its progressive-disclosure memory pattern: compact search results first, timeline/context second, full observations only for filtered IDs.
use_for: Persistent memory design across agent sessions
avoid_for: Capturing secrets, Vault output, Telegram private messages, or raw trading logs
Auto-injecting large historical context into every prompt
Replacing local deterministic validators
required_guardrails: Use private/exclusion controls for secrets and user data
Store compact summaries rather than full transcripts
Retrieve by IDs and batch only the necessary observations

projects.name: Everything Claude Code
projects.token_strategy: Borrow only selected Codex-compatible skills and AGENTS.md patterns; do not bulk-import the full skill pack into this repository.
use_for: Reusable security and documentation review checklists
avoid_for: Vendoring every skill, command, hook, and MCP server
Loading the whole upstream repo as context
Applying generic frontend/content skills to trading execution code
install: Review upstream skills first; copy only the specific skill needed
Keep project-local Codex instructions in AGENTS.md and .codex/
required_guardrails: Selective adoption only
Preserve this repo's trading risk controls as the source of truth
Run tests after importing any workflow rule that changes agent behavior

projects.name: Superpowers
projects.token_strategy: Use as a lightweight methodology reference for spec-first, plan-first, TDD, and review loops; keep the active context to the current task and touched files.
use_for: Reducing rework by clarifying scope before broad edits
avoid_for: Mandatory process overhead for small fixes
Replacing repo-specific MCP/project context tools
Expanding task scope beyond the user's request
install: Use upstream methodology as reference; do not install runtime dependencies by default
required_guardrails: Keep plans short and scoped
Prefer existing tests and narrow new tests
Do not bypass execution-mode, ledger, or risk guardrails

projects.name: Ruflo
projects.token_strategy: Use Ruflo-style orchestration only for bounded, parallel research or review tasks with strict per-agent file ownership and compact synthesis.
use_for: Parallel code review or research planning
avoid_for: Autonomous live trading actions
Unbounded swarms over the full repository
Sending secrets, private Telegram content, or raw ledger data to external agents
required_guardrails: Bound agent count, scope, and file ownership
Require one compact synthesis before implementation
Treat all outputs as advisory until local tests and risk checks pass

projects.name: Market Intelligence Platform App
projects.token_strategy: Use the local Python adapter and reports instead of loading the upstream app. Start with utils/crypto_market_intelligence.py and scripts/crypto_market_intelligence.py.
use_for: Advisory market reports for Telegram, API, or CLI
avoid_for: Direct trading execution
Replacing parser, ledger, risk, HMM, or execution-mode guardrails
Treating ranked opportunities as orders without deterministic validation
Sending secrets or private Telegram data to external services
required_guardrails: Advisory only by default
Use PolymarketClient public market data; no secrets required
All generated signals must pass parser, risk, ledger, and execution mode checks before action
Keep reports compact for Telegram and LLM prompts

projects.name: OpenBB
projects.token_strategy: Use OpenBB as a reference for market data aggregation, research workflows, and AI-agent tooling. Prefer compact adapters and context cards instead of vendoring upstream code.
use_for: Reference design for external data backends
avoid_for: Direct trading execution
Vendoring AGPL code into the runtime without license review
Replacing the existing risk, ledger, or execution guardrails
Sending secrets or private data to external OpenBB services
required_guardrails: Treat OpenBB output as research input only until validated locally
Keep the integration as an adapter or context card, not a runtime replacement
Add focused tests for any adapter or MCP tool that consumes OpenBB data
Keep all execution decisions behind parser, risk, ledger, and execution-mode checks

projects.name: GSD / get-shit-done
projects.license: MIT upstream; adapted as concepts/config/docs only.
projects.token_strategy: Use config/gsd_operating_system.json to build compact work packets before loading broad repo context. Keep the phase report short and verify required outputs before handoff.
use_for: Verification gates for agentic code changes
avoid_for: Vendoring the upstream runtime into this repo
Bypassing local Codex permissions or production guardrails
Replacing Lobstar risk, ledger, credential, or execution controls
Sending secrets, wallet data, Telegram data, or raw logs to external LLMs
required_guardrails: Adapters and context cards only by default
Every phase must define required outputs
Every implementation must include focused verification or residual-risk notes
No live trading action without deterministic parser, risk, ledger, and MODE validation

projects.name: Multi-Persona Prompt Registry
projects.token_strategy: Use utils.persona_prompts.build_multi_persona_packet to generate compact persona prompts and priority files instead of pasting large role descriptions into every prompt.
use_for: Trader, coder, ML, strategist, analyst, and actuary reviews
avoid_for: Live trading execution
Sending secrets, wallet data, Telegram data, or raw logs to external models
Unbounded multi-agent fan-out without a synthesis contract
required_guardrails: Run personas independently before synthesis
Preserve dissent and uncertainty
Keep MiroFish research-only and Ruflo bounded by file ownership
Require deterministic verification before action
